steps.title: Name the simile's job
steps.content: Bartley has been complaining that misfortune always falls on him. The underlined comparison to crows descending on seed potatoes gives a concrete image for that complaint.

steps.title: Rule out literal readings
steps.content: Choice A incorrectly shifts the idea to Mrs. Tarpey. Choice C calls the simile a counterargument, but it intensifies Bartley's own claim. Choice D treats figurative language as a literal scene.

steps.title: Choose vivid emphasis
steps.content: The underlined portion presents a vivid image that emphasizes Bartley's claim about misfortune targeting him. The answer is B.
